summary: The erdos_problems dataset tracks 1,217 problems (1,179 open) from Paul Erdős, maintained by Terence Tao. Current activity is concentrated in a small sample: erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 each have one resolved proof issue, while quarterly buckets q1-q10 show 100 issues each, suggesting bulk or systematically generated tracking rather than organic per-problem traction. Available metadata is sparse, limiting precise identification of high-traction individual problems.
problems: erdos_0001: single proof issue, now resolved (untitled)
erdos_0002: single proof issue, now resolved (untitled)
q1-q10: 100 issues each with no per-problem breakdown, indicating possible batch import or unlabeled tracking that needs triage
Most of the 1,179 open problems have no associated issue activity recorded in this context
Resolved proof issues lack descriptive titles, hampering review and verification
highlights: erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 each have a completed proof issue, demonstrating the resolution workflow functions end-to-end
Quarterly buckets (q1-q10) collectively contain 1,000 issues, indicating substantial cataloging effort already underway
Dataset is well-structured with tags, OEIS references, cash prizes, and clear open/solved/disproved status to support prioritization
Maintenance by Terence Tao lends authoritative status tracking that can be leveraged for verification

suggested_todos.title: Triage the 100-issue q1-q10 buckets to map issues back to specific Erdős problem IDs
Add descriptive titles to the resolved proof issues for erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 to enable peer review
Identify problems with cash prizes and existing OEIS references as near-term solvable candidates
Establish a tagging convention to distinguish organic activity from batch-imported issues
Generate an activity ranking once per-problem issue counts are extracted from the quarterly buckets
